change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, photographs, printed material, publications, videos, plaques
did.abstract: Memorabilia and papers of Samuel R. Pierce from his Treasury Board appointment in 1970 to his later years as Secretary of the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Sean Lee, Fall 2011Celena Huo, Anne Jones, April 2012

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Samuel R. Pierce was born September 8, 1922 in Glen Cove, New York, and served in the U.S. Army during and after the Second World War (1943-1946). He obtained his A.B. with honors from Cornell (1947), his law degree in 1949 from the Cornell Law School and earned his LL.M. in taxation (1952) at New York University School of Law. After being admitted to the New York Bar in 1949 he served as assistant district attorney of New York (1949-1953). Pierce was first lieutenant judge advocate general corps, reserve (1950-1952), assistant U.S. attorney, Southern District New York (1953-1955), assistant undersecretary of the Department of Labor during President Eisenhower's first term (1955-1956), and he served as associate counsel of the Council of the Judicial Subcommittee on Anti-Trust for the U.S. Supreme Court (1956). For a brief time, Pierce was a member of the U.S. House of Representatives (1956-1957). He then earned his J.D. and conducted postgraduate work at Yale Law School from 1957 to 1958. Pierce also served as a judge of the New York Court of General Sessions (1959-1961). He was very active in practicing private law, doing so on and off for well over twenty years (1957-1959, 1961-1970, and 1973-1981), at one time becoming a partner of Battle, Fowler, Pierce and Kheel. He was also on the faculty of New York University from 1958 to 1970. Pierce worked as general counsel and head legal director of the U.S. Department of the Treasury from 1970 to 1973 and served as Secretary of Housing and Urban Development in the Reagan Administration from 1981 to 1989. He was the first African American to serve in Ronald Reagan's Cabinet. Pierce died on October 31st, 2000.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Memorabilia, plaques, degrees, awards and recognitions, appointment books, scrapbooks, speech transcripts, biographies, flyers, clippings, photographs, audio and video tapes documenting the career of Samuel R. Pierce from his Treasury Board appointment in 1970 to his later years as Secretary of the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD). Includes correspondence relating to his legal career, Treasury papers and HUD records. Also court case studies, academic publications, magazines, calendars, booklets, pamphlets, news articles, government documents (HUD and Treasury related), scrapbooks, ceremony and event ephemera, federal orders, and Boy Scout badges.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series III. Department of Treasury Box 5
Series IV. Department of Housing and Urban Development Box 5-7, 42
Series XI. Awards and Gifts Box 22-27, 30, 34-47, Mapcase

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Samuel R. Pierce papers, #6531. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: For educational/research use only. Publication beyond the limit of fair use requires permission.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Fixed typos; Appointment books found in box 4.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Framed items 1-10 are closed until processed.

Framed copy of H.R. 1158 with photograph of Ronald Reagan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of the act that provided for the amendment of the 1968 Civil Rights Act to revise procedures for the enforcement of fair housing, and for other purposes. The frame also includes a photo of President Reagan with Pierce. The back states that this was presented to Pierce for his eight years of leadership in the campaign to guarantee equal housing opportunities for all Americans. It is from and signed by J. Michael Dorsey, John J. Knapp, Judith Y. Brachman, Antonio Monroig, Thomas D. Casey, Deborah Gore Dean, Lance H. Wilson, and one other.

did.unittitle: Award: The Quarterback Club of Washington, D.C. (Trophy)
Medals: The Department of Treasury - Secretaries of Treasury During Nixon Administration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Richard Nixon, George P. Shultz, John B. Connally, David M. Kennedy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reagan's Ruling Class, The Autobiography of G. Gordon Liddy, U.S.S. Intrepid, A Decent Place to Live: The Report of the National Housing Task Force, The Journal of the Institute for Socioeconomic Studies, Cityscape: A Journal of Policy Development and Research, The Hoover Institution: Report 1984

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Heritage Lectures, Reagan's Report Card (article), Weekly Compilation of Presidential Documents, bills, receipts, invitations, informational catalogues, advertisements, a card containing names and numbers of the Treasury officials, Radio Corporation of America stock information booklet
